assignments.instructionText: Resume Assignment Instructions Part 1: Create/Update Your Resume Step 1: Choose a job postingSelect a co-op/internship job posting that is appropriate to your program. Step 2: Analyze the job postingRead through the posting carefully and identify the key skills, qualifications, and requirements the employer is looking for. Step 3: Create your resumeUsing Microsoft Word, create/update your resume to match the job you selected. Refer to the Resume section in this SLATE course and the resume lecture for guidance. Part 2: Optimize Your Resume with AI Step 1: Log in to SkillsFirstGo to skillsfirst.com/organizations/sheridan-college and log in using your Sheridan College email and password. Step 2: Learn how to use SkillsFirstWatch this tutorial to understand how the system works:→ SkillsFirst Video TutorialOR read the step-by-step guide:→ SkillsFirst User Guide (PDF) Step 3: Upload your resumeUpload your resume to SkillsFirst and read the feedback it gives you. Step 4: Improve your resumeMake changes to your resume based on the AI feedback. Step 5: Get your scoreClick the blue "Enhance with AI" button (on the right side)Then click "Canadian Resume Readiness Check"Then click "Run AI Analysis"The system will give you a score. If your score is "Good" or higher:Great job! Take screenshots of the entire AI Report (make sure your name is visible in the screenshots). You will submit these screenshots with your assignment. If your score is "Needs Improvement":Make more changes to your resume based on the feedback. Upload your updated resume to SkillsFirst again and run the analysis again. Keep doing this until you get "Good" or higher. Then take the screenshots to submit. Part 3: What to Submit You need to submit 3 documents: Item 1: Job PostingSave as a Word document (.doc or .docx) Item 2: Your Final ResumeSave as a PDFFile name must be: FirstName-LastName-Resume Final(Example: John-Smith-Resume Final.pdf) Item 3: SkillsFirst ScreenshotsScreenshots of your AI ReportImportant: Your name must be visible in the screenshots Remember: You need at least 70% to pass this assignment. Put the required effort into creating a thoughtful, tailored resume that showcases your skills and demonstrates why you're the perfect fit for the position!
assignments.Name: Resume Assignment

assignments.instructionText: Cover Letter Assignment Instructions Part 1: Create Your Cover Letter Step 1: Review your job postingUse the same job posting you selected for your resume assignment. Read through it again carefully. Step 2: Write your cover letterCreate a cover letter that complements your tailored resume. Highlight your qualifications, skills, and experiences that match the requirements of the job posting. Step 3: Match your resume designUse a similar design and formatting style for your cover letter as you did for your resume. This ensures consistency in your job application package. ⚠️ VERY IMPORTANT - READ CAREFULLY: You are NOT allowed to use AI tools (like ChatGPT) to write your cover letter for this assignment. Generic, AI-generated cover letters are easy to spot and employers can tell when you haven't written it yourself. This is your chance to show your personality and genuine interest in the company. You must write your own cover letter in your own words. Part 2: Review Your Cover Letter Step 1: Use the Cover Letter ChecklistDownload and complete the Cover Letter Checklist. This checklist ensures you have included all the necessary components in your cover letter. You should be able to confidently check "yes" for each item. Step 2: Proofread & save as a PDF Double-check that you have used appropriate language, grammar, and spelling. Your cover letter must be error-free. Once finalized, save your cover letter as a PDF. Pro Tip: Your cover letter MUST fit on one page and MUST match the formatting/designs used in your resume. Part 3: What to Submit You need to submit 2 documents: Item 1: Your Cover LetterSave as a PDFFile name should be: FirstName-LastName-Cover Letter(Example: John-Smith-Cover Letter.pdf) Item 2: Completed Cover Letter ChecklistMake sure you have checked off each item on the checklist Remember: You need at least 70% to pass this assignment. Put the required effort into creating a thoughtful, personalized cover letter that shows employers who you are and why you're the perfect fit!
assignments.Name: Cover Letter Assignment

assignments.instructionText: Mock Interview Assignment Instructions This is NOT a regular dropbox - you cannot submit files here. This page only contains instructions. Your actual interview will be recorded in a different location: the Virtual Assignment - Video Mock Interview. That system will automatically record and submit your interview for you. Where to find your grade: After your interview is assessed, check the SLATE Gradebook (not this page). Part 1: Prepare for Your Interview Step 1: Review your job postingUse the same job posting you selected for your resume and cover letter assignments. Step 2: Prepare to answer these 5 questionsYou will need to record your answers to the following five interview questions:1. Tell me about yourself2. What interests you about this role (position and company)?3. What is your greatest strength? Tell me about a time you used that skill in a previous position.4. Describe a time when you had to handle several priorities at once. How did you stay organized?5. Do you have any questions for us? Step 3: Practice first (ungraded)Before doing the real interview, practice in the Video Mock Interview Practice Space. This practice space is identical to the real interview environment but will not be graded. Use this to get comfortable with the system. Part 2: Record Your Graded Interview The system will automatically submit 2 items: Item 1: Your Mock Interview RecordingWhen you're ready, go to the official Video Mock Interview Space. There are 5 interview questions. Record your answers, being mindful of your body language and overall performance. Item 2: Your Reflection ResponsesAfter answering the questions, please include a reflection on your performance directly in the video. Discuss what you think you did well and areas for improvement. Remember: Practice in the ungraded practice space first! This helps you get comfortable with the system before doing your official graded interview. When you press the record button, the software starts recording immediately. You CANNOT pause, stop, rewind, redo, or delete your answer once you start. Each question automatically submits after you finish recording it. You can take a break between questions, but once you start a question, you must complete it in one take. Make sure you're ready before pressing record!
assignments.Name: Interview Assignment (Instructions)

assignments.instructionText: Important note: If you already have your coop work-permit then you don't not need to complete this task Task: Email the confirmation document you received from IRCC after applying for your co-op work permit to coopworkpermit@sheridancollege.ca. Please see an example of what that document looks by clicking here. Instructions: Apply for your co-op work permit through IRCC if you haven't already. Once you receive a confirmation from IRCC, email it to coopworkpermit@sheridancollege.ca. Need Help?For more information on the co-op work permit, visit here. If you need assistance, contact an International Student Advisor at Sheridan here.
assignments.Name: Co-op Work Permit Application Proof  - IF YOU HAVE YOUR COOP WORK-PERMIT YOU DON'T NEED TO DO THIS

Attachments.attachmentText: Date: Application no.: W … … … . . Please quote this reference number when referring to this application. Dear This confirms that your application has been received by Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) on (yyyy/mm/dd). When will IRCC contact me? You are expected to provide a COPY of your passport with your electronic application. If IRCC needs your physical passport, IRCC will request that you submit it to our office. IRCC will also contact you if we require additional information, documents or an interview. For general guidance on your application visit My immigration and citizenship application or consult the “What Happens Next” section of the instruction guide associated with the application form you used. Please do not send to IRCC any documentation related to this online application by mail, courier or fax. Any mail received, other than what is received in your account, will NOT be considered. How long will it take to process my application? Processing times vary. Please refer to the following page for processing times related to your application: http://www.cic.gc.ca/english/information/times/index.asp. CAUTION: Do not submit multiple applications for the same service in an effort to speed up your application. Doing this may result in both applications being processed and delay the finalization of your application. IRCC does not refund processed applications. Unless your application has exceeded normal processing times, please limit your correspondence to notifying us of changes in your application. For additional information, consult the IRCC website at www.cic.gc.ca. Citizenship and Immigration Canada IMM5739 E (02-2021) GCMS https://www.canada.ca/en/immigration-refugees-citizenship/services/application.html http://www.cic.gc.ca/english/information/times/index.asp http://www.cic.gc.ca/

assignments.instructionText: Email a copy of your co-op work permit it coopworkpermit@sheridancollege.ca
assignments.Name: Copy of Co-op Work Permit (International Students Only)

assignments.instructionText:
Student Survey Assignment Instructions

Part 1: Complete the Survey

Step 1: Access the surveyGo to the survey link: Student Co-op Survey
Step 2: Complete the surveyTake your time and carefully complete the survey. It should take about 15-30 minutes to finish. Reflect on your co-op or internship goals as you answer the questions.
Step 3: Check your email for resultsAfter completing the survey, you will receive a copy of the results in your inbox. This may take a few minutes to arrive.

Part 2: What to Submit

You need to submit 1 item:
Item 1: Your FULL Survey ResultsDownload the complete survey results from your email inbox and upload them to this dropbox.
assignments.Name: Student Survey
